commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No repair lane is needed; the remaining action is normal maintainer review, CI, and explicit comfort with the auth-provider endpoint change.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue found; the diff changes one provider's HTTPS request/parsing path plus tests and docs without adding dependencies, scripts, or broader secret access. (sha=56d2cdd7c13c0aec0b3840613067277d8b8dd04b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR switches Alibaba Token Plan usage fetching to Bailian `GetSubscriptionSummary`, updates quota parsing and tests, and adds Token Plan provider docs.

Reproducibility: no. I did not reproduce the live failure against current main. Source inspection shows current main still calls the old token-plan instance gateway, and the inspected PR proof shows the replacement Bailian endpoint returning the expected live fields.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed; the remaining action is normal maintainer review, CI, and explicit comfort with the auth-provider endpoint change.

target.title: fix: update Alibaba Token Plan usage API
target.body: ## Summary - Switch Alibaba Token Plan usage fetching to Bailian `GetSubscriptionSummary` - Update token-plan parsing for subscription summary quota fields, remaining credits, expiry date, and empty subscription state - Refresh Alibaba Token Plan tests and provider docs for the new endpoint ## Background Alibaba Token Plan changed its official usage API during this weekend, so the previous token-plan instance query endpoint is no longer suitable as the source of current usage data. This PR moves the fetcher to the new Bailian subscription summary endpoint to keep Token Plan credit usage working. ## Validation - `swift test --filter AlibabaTokenPlan` - `swiftformat Sources Tests --lint` - `swiftlint lint --strict --no-cache --quiet` ## Proof Verified against a real Alibaba Token Plan session. Redacted live API check: ```bash curl 'https://bailian.console.aliyun.com/data/api.json?action=GetSubscriptionSummary&product=BssOpenAPI-V3&_tag=' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \ -H 'Cookie: ' \ --data 'product=BssOpenAPI-V3&action=GetSubscriptionSummary¶ms=%7B%22ProductCode%22%3A%22sfm_tokenplanteams_dp_cn%22%7D®ion=cn-beijing' ``` Note: `make check` reached SwiftFormat/SwiftLint with 0 violations, then failed only because the sandbox blocked a macOS plist cache write.
target.author: yanxinxue

commands.author: YanxinXue
